channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Teen charged with violent extremist offences allegedly planned to threaten school
item.description: Queensland Police say a 13-year-old boy posed an "imminent threat" to a Maryborough school.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: The boy will reappear in the Hervey Bay Magistrates Court on Friday.

item.title: 'Panic started to set in': Woman rescued from quicksand on Adelaide beach
item.description: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Haynes and Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Madz June's calf was submerged in sand at Glenelg North beach.

item.title: Police investigate nine-week-old baby's 'serious' head injuries
item.description: Queensland Police are investigating after a nine-week-old baby was taken to hospital earlier this year with "serious" head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Madeline Grace

media:group.media:description: An injured baby was taken to the Sunshine Coast University Hospital earlier this year.

item.title: Iran and US both say they targeted the other's military
item.description: The United States says it struck Iranian military and Iran says it responded by targeting a US base.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump says Iran "really wants to make a deal", but sticking points between the US and Iran remain.

item.title: Beauty queen's accused killer allegedly told friends immigration took her
item.description: The murder trial of Mark Sheridan Waden has heard allegations he killed his then-partner Priscilla Brooten during an argument almost eight years ago, after the American former beauty queen learned he was having an affair with a colleague.
item.dc:creator: Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: Priscilla Brooten was allegedly murdered in July 2018.

item.title: New Wieambilla property owners send message about respecting the lives lost
item.description: The son of Wieambilla police killers says the new owners of the property are open to maintaining a memorial at the site to the slain officers.
item.dc:creator: Cindy Wockner

media:group.media:description: Matthew Arnold and Rachel McCrow were killed at the property in 2022.

item.title: Euphoria season 3 finale recap: Guns, drugs and Rue's fate
item.description: Here's your recap of season 3, episode 8 of Euphoria. There are spoilers. You have been warned.
item.dc:creator: Mawunyo Gbogbo

media:group.media:description: There has been speculation over the fate of Rue Bennett (Zendaya) in Euphoria.

item.title: Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog to get follow-the-money powers
item.description: The new powers will allow IBAC to pursue taxpayer funds through subcontracting arrangements and labour-hire companies.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: Jacinta Allan says the overhauls are the most extensive since IBAC was established.

item.title: Maroons hit with double injury blow ahead of Origin II
item.description: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

media:group.media:description: Pat Carrigan (left) and Gehamat Shibasaki are unavailable for State of Origin II.

item.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over hospital aerosol can death
item.description: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: The 22-year-old was an in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital when they died.

item.title: Australia won't impose travel restrictions despite overseas Ebola spread
item.description: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Dalzell and Paige Cockburn

media:group.media:description: There have been more than 220 likely Ebola-related deaths in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: School students buck trend to stage their own live music festival
item.description: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.
item.dc:creator: Shauna Foley

media:group.media:description: Misanthropy were the openers for the student music festival.

item.title: Police scour mine shafts, drain dam in search for missing outback woman
item.description: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

media:group.media:description: Trisha Graf was last seen in the early hours of December 12, 2025.

item.title: 'One more to go': Aussies on cusp of winning rugby 7s World Championship
item.description: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australia enjoyed the spoils of victory after defeating USA in the women's final.

item.title: $6 million of cocaine found at north Queensland boat ramp
item.description: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell

media:group.media:description: The suspicious packages were found to contain cocaine.

item.title: Greyhound ban bill won't go to vote until 'we know it will pass', Rockliff says
item.description: A bill to ban greyhound racing in Tasmania has been delayed until the government can secure enough support to pass it. Meanwhile, financial support has been offered to racing participants ahead of the 2029 phase-out deadline.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Jaggers

media:group.media:description: A bill to phase out the greyhound racing industry still needs to pass Tasmania's upper house of parliament to come into effect.

item.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy
item.description: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".
item.dc:creator: Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: A Welcome to Country performed in Boorloo (Perth) as part of Reconciliation Week.

item.title: Four easy, tasty curries for busy winter weeknights
item.description: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: A plate of pumpkin, lentil and coconut curry, topped with coconut cream and tadka. Slow cooker visible with more curry.

item.title: South Australia braces for severe weather and damaging winds
item.description: The State Emergency Service has advised people to move vehicles away from trees and secure loose items around properties as South Australia braces for damaging winds.

media:group.media:description: Damaging wind gusts, showers and storms are expected across South Australia from Monday

item.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life
item.description: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Pearce

media:group.media:description: The PS Canally has been recommissioned as a commercial vessel.

item.title: Beneath the rubble of Liddell Power Station towers lay 'perfect' plan
item.description: Half a tonne of explosives was needed to bring the Hunter Valley's Liddell Power Station chimneys crashing down.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Bernasconi and Courtney Yeandle

media:group.media:description: The two chimneys have been part of the Hunter region's skyline for more than half a century.

item.title: Israel laid siege to an 889-year-old castle. Here's why that matters
item.description: Israel has captured a castle in southern Lebanon in a mountainous military siege that marked Israeli forces' deepest incursion into the country in 26 years.
item.dc:creator: Luke Cooper

media:group.media:description: Israeli troops have raised the Israeli flag (right) and the Golani flag, representing the 1982 capture, over the castle.

item.title: Chadstone cold case accused faces court after 30 years
item.description: A 78-year-old man accused of shooting three people in an Armaguard robbery at a busy cinema in 1994 faces court.

media:group.media:description: Two Armaguard employees were shot after they collected the weekend takings from a cinema.

item.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother
item.description: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Bo Sebastian De-Simone has admitted murdering his mother.

item.title: Eerie footage and DNA: What we know four months since Nancy Guthrie vanished
item.description: It has been four months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of America's Today show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. This is what we know about the alleged abduction.

media:group.media:description: Savannah Guthrie and her mother Nancy, who was last seen on January 31.
